claims.claim_text: A 40-year field experiment found winter wheat grain yield was highest after legume preceding crops (5.4 t/ha), intermediate after oil plants (4.7 t/ha), and lowest after cereals (4.1 t/ha), quantifying the yield advantage of a legume before a cereal.
claims.evidence_text: The influence of the preceding crop on wheat grain yield was found to be significant between 1979 and 2018, with grain yields of 5.4 ± 1.8 t/ha after legumes, 4.7 ± 2.0 t/ha after oil plants, and 4.1 ± 1.8 t/ha after cereals.

value.raw_value_text: wheat after legumes 5.4 vs after cereals 4.1 t/ha

provenance.source_title: The Evaluation of a Long-Term Experiment on the Relationships between Weather, Nitrogen Fertilization, Preceding Crop, and Winter Wheat Grain Yield on Cambisol
